title: byrsonima japurensis subsp. fluminensis
intro: Byrsonima japurensis subsp. fluminensis, also known as the Fluminense tree, is a species of plant in the family Malpighiaceae, native to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Byrsonima japurensis subsp. fluminensis include the potential treatment of various conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and capsules for medicinal purposes.
